QF.id: XIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_15

question.text: What is the maximum contract duration available for equity index futures vs currency futures on Indian exchanges?

answer.text: Equity index: 3 monthly contracts (near, mid, far). Currency (USDINR): up to 12 monthly contracts
Equity index: 1 month only. Currency: 6 months

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Equity index futures (NIFTY/SENSEX) have 3 serial monthly contracts plus weekly expiries. USDINR currency futures extend up to 12 months. This difference reflects the different hedging horizons — forex hedgers often need longer-term coverage for trade exposures. **The Trap:** The availability of weeklies in equity doesn't extend the monthly horizon beyond 3 months. Currency futures offering 12 months doesn't mean all months are equally liquid. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch3 covers equity futures contract cycle. Series I Ch3 covers currency futures contract cycle. This comparison is a key Series XIII topic. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Currency futures' longer availability (12 months) reflects the needs of exporters/importers with longer receivable/payable cycles — equity hedgers typically need shorter horizons.
